strategies.summary: Multiple feature pairs exhibit perfect or near-perfect correlation (correlation=1 or ~0.9993), indicating strong redundancy and deterministic relationships among packet length, segment size, header length, flag counts, and packet counts in both forward and backward directions.
strategies.strategic_objective: Clarify the nature, stability, and class-conditioning of strong feature dependencies to assess redundancy and dimensionality reduction potential.

strategies.summary: Backward packet length features (Max, Mean, Std) and Avg Bwd Segment Size show significant class-conditioned structural variation with high variance ratios and large differences in unique values between BENIGN and DDoS classes, indicating potential discriminative power.
strategies.strategic_objective: Investigate the stability and contextual sensitivity of class-conditioned variations in backward packet length features to understand their discriminative reliability.

strategies.summary: Destination Port feature shows strong distributional differences between BENIGN and DDoS classes, with DDoS having a dominant ratio of 1.0 and zero entropy, while BENIGN has lower dominant ratio and higher entropy, resulting in high Jensen-Shannon divergence, indicating representation sensitivity to class-conditioned shifts.
strategies.strategic_objective: Elucidate the causes and stability of the zero entropy distribution in DDoS destination ports and its potential as a representation-sensitive detection signal.
